expectations.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section
expectations.evidence: Has a 'Context' section with basic info (source, target, consumers, constraints) but lacks the structured Decomposition Summary with Workstreams, Highest-Risk Changes, Assumptions, Open Questions, and Execution Waves

expectations.text: Tasks are organized into execution waves
expectations.evidence: Has a dependency graph and suggested timeline at the end, but tasks are not organized into named execution waves with dependency references. Uses sequential task numbering with a dependency graph.

expectations.text: Every task card includes required fields: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands
expectations.evidence: Cards include Goal, Scope, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Steps. Missing: In Scope/Non-Goals distinction, Context Anchor, Parallelizable, Can Land Independently, Change Type, Risk Level, Compatibility (API/Data/Rollout), Verification Prerequisites, Constraints, Rollout/Rollback Notes, Scope Check

expectations.text: Migration is split into separate cards for schema setup, dual-write, backfill, read cutover, and cleanup
expectations.evidence: Task 2 (schema), Task 3 (DMS replication), Task 5 (dual-write), Task 6 (validation), Task 7 (read cutover), Task 8 (write cutover), Task 10 (monitoring)

expectations.text: High-risk migration cards include rollback notes
expectations.evidence: Some tasks mention rollback capability within scope descriptions, but there's no dedicated rollback/rollout section per card. Less structured than skill output.

expectations.text: Accounts for all 3 services needing updates
expectations.evidence: Task 4 audits all 3 services. Task 5 implements dual-write across all services. Not split per-service though.

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses zero-downtime with a staged approach
expectations.evidence: DMS continuous replication, incremental read traffic shift (5% -> 25% -> 100%), staged approach throughout

expectations.text: Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section
expectations.evidence: No dedicated Follow-up Tasks section. All tasks are treated as required.
